papers.title_en: Divide, Deliberate, Decide: A Multi-Agent Framework for Fine-Grained Egocentric Action Recognition
papers.authors: Alessandro Sottovia, Alessandro Torcinovich, Oswald Lanz
papers.evidence: 通过多智能体VLM协商实现零样本动作识别
papers.tldr: 自我中心视频中细粒度动作识别因微小视觉线索差异而面临挑战，单一VLM模型易产生偏差。本文提出Divide, Deliberate, Decide框架，由编排器分块提案、异构VLM专家审议并聚合排名，无需微调即可零样本推理。实验表明该方法显著提升识别性能，增益来源于异构模型先验的去相关性而非额外计算。该框架为本地化零样本动作识别提供了有效范式。
papers.abstract_en: Fine-grained action recognition in egocentric video is challenging for Vision-Language Models (VLMs): actions often differ only in small visual cues, and a single model tends to be biased toward a subset of these cues. We propose Divide, Deliberate, Decide, a fully-local, zero-shot multi-agent framework in which (i) a VLM orchestrator chunks the video and proposes a top-k candidate label list per segment, (ii) an ensemble of heterogeneous VLM specialists, drawn from different open model families, engages in a structured deliberation that includes a peer-consultation round of questions, and (iii) agent rankings are aggregated with a Borda count and the orchestrator re-ranks its own prediction in light of the specialists' evidence. The entire pipeline runs locally with no fine-tuning. Experiments show that our method positively improves zero-shot action recognition performance over the baseline, highlighting the influence of a heterogeneous deliberation step, showing that the gain stems from decorrelated model priors rather than from additional compute.

papers.title_en: Cross-Modal Masked Compositional Concept Modeling for Enhancing Visio-Linguistic Compositionality
papers.authors: Wei Li, Zhen Huang, Xinmei Tian
papers.evidence: 跨模态掩码组合概念建模，可用于组合动作识别方法
papers.tldr: 针对视觉语言模型在组合理解上的“词袋”问题，本文提出MACCO框架，通过跨模态掩码组合概念建模，即掩码一个模态中的组合概念并从另一模态完整上下文重建，并引入跨模态和模态内对齐损失，有效增强模型的组合性，在五个基准上取得显著提升，并惠及文本到图像生成和多模态大语言模型。
papers.abstract_en: Contrastively trained vision-language models like CLIP, have made remarkable progress in learning joint image-text representations, but still face challenges in compositional understanding. They often exhibit a "bag-of-words" behavior--struggling to capture the object relations, attribute-object bindings, and word order dependencies. This limitation arises not only from the reliance on global, single-vector representations for optimization, but also from the insufficient exploitation and modeling of the rich compositional information inherently present in paired image text data. In this work, we propose MACCO (MAsked Compositional Concept MOdeling), a framework that masks compositional concepts in one modality and reconstructs them conditioned on the full contextual information from the other, enabling the model to capture and align cross-modal compositional structures more effectively. To facilitate this process, we introduce two auxiliary objectives that jointly align and regularize masked features both inter-modally and intra-modally. Extensive experiments on five compositional benchmarks, along with in-depth analyses, demonstrate that our approach not only significantly enhances compositionality in VLMs but also improves their ability to capture syntactic structure and linguistic information. Additionally, the improved compositionality also benefits text-to-image generation and multimodal large language model. Code is available at https://github.com/hiker-lw/MACCO.
